revision.comment: Bot: remove [[Category:Sn crud]] (crud category cleanup) [[github:EmmaLeonhart/shintowiki-scripts/actions/runs/23986660476|commit triggered pipeline]]
revision.text:
[[ja:市庭古墳]] {{Infobox ancient site|type = [[Zenpokoenfun]] |name = Ichiniwa Kofun |image = File:Ichiniwa kohun aerial.jpg |caption = The outer moat is highlighted in black. The area below is the site of the Daigokuden. {{Aerial Photography of National Land}} | part_of = {{ill|Saki-Tatenami Kofun Cluster|qid=Q11383489|1=Saki Tatenami Kofun group}} |location = {{ill|Nara|qid=Q169134|1=Nara (city)}} |coordinates = {{Coord|34.696542|N|135.797186|E|region:JP_type:landmark|display=inline,title}} |type = Keyhole-shaped kofun (front portion destroyed) |area = Mound length: 253 m (reconstructed) |burial = |built = Early 5th century |Occupant = Designated by the {{ill|Imperial Household Agency|en|Imperial Household Agency|qid=Q1143602|lt=Imperial Household Agency|1=Imperial Household Agency}} as Emperor Heizei (Yamamomo no misasagi) |burial goods = |Mausoleum = Designated as "Yamamomo no misasagi" by the Imperial Household Agency |notes = 14th largest kofun in Japan
(with a length of 250 m)[https://www.city.sakai.lg.jp/kanko/rekishi/dkofun/Ranking/zenkoku.html Kofun area Ranking (National)] (Sakai City Official Website, Updated May 13, 2018). |map = {{location map|Japan Nara|coordinates=34|coordinates=41|coordinates=47.55|lon_deg=135|lon_min=47|lon_sec=49.87|map_dot_label=Ichiniwa Kofun|mark=前方後円墳-南.svg|marksize=23|caption=location in Nara Prefecture|position=bottom|width=200|float=center|border=none}} {{maplink2|type=point|frame=yes|plain=yes|zoom=14|frame-align=center|frame-width=200|coord={{coord|34|41|47.55|N|135|47|49.87|E}}}} |architectural_styles = {{ill|keyhole-shaped kofun|qid=Q11268718|1=Zenpōkōenfun|2=ja|3=前方後円墳|4=ko|5=전방후원분|6=zh|7=前方後圓墳}} |commemorates = {{ill|Emperor Heizei|qid=Q353558|1=Emperor Heizei}} }} {{panoViewer|Saki Ichiniwa Kofun, Haisho 360-degree.jpg|Yamamomo no misasagi {{ill|Haisho|ja|拝所|lt=Haisho|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Haisho|qq=}}, 360-degree panoramic image}} '''Ichiniwa Kofun''' ({{lang|ja|市庭古墳|1=Ichiniba Kofun}}) is a {{ill|Zenpokoenfun|en|Zenpokoenfun|qid=Q11268718|lt=Zenpokoenfun|1=Zenpōkōenfun}} located in Saki-cho, {{ill|Nara (city)|en|Nara (city)|qid=Q169134|lt=Nara|1=Nara (city)}}, Nara Prefecture, Japan. It is part of the {{ill|Saki Tatenami Kofun Group|ja|佐紀盾列古墳群|1=Saki Tatenami Kofun group|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Saki Tatenami Kofun Group|13=User:Immanuelle/Saki Tatenami Kofun group|qq=}}. Although the actual Interred_individual remains unknown, the {{ill|Imperial Household Agency|en|Imperial Household Agency|qid=Q1143602|lt=Imperial Household Agency|1=Imperial Household Agency}} has Historic Site1 it as "'''Yamamomo no misasagi'''" (楊梅陵), the tomb of the 51st Emperor of Japan, Emperor Heizei. This kofun belongs to the eastern section of the Saki Tatenami Kofun Group, located at its westernmost end. Although originally keyhole-shaped, the front portion has been destroyed, leaving only part of the rear circular mound. == Overview == [[File:Ichiniwa Kofun.jpg|thumb|left|200px|Illustration of the destroyed sections. Created by the {{ill|Nara National Research Institute for Cultural Properties|en|Nara National Research Institute for Cultural Properties|qid=Q3297691|lt=Nara National Research Institute for Historic Site|1=Nara National Research Institute for Cultural Properties}}.]] The kofun is situated immediately north of the remains of the {{ill|Daigokuden|ja|大極殿|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Daigokuden|qq=}} at the {{ill|Heijō Palace|en|Heijō Palace|qid=Q211585|lt=Heijō Palace|1=Heijō Palace}} site. Initially thought to be one of the largest circular mounds with a diameter exceeding 100 meters, later excavations revealed that its front portion was leveled during the construction of Heijō Palace. The kofun was originally a keyhole-shaped mound oriented southward, with an estimated length of 253 meters, a front width of 164 meters, and a rear circular diameter of 147 meters. Remains of {{ill|Fukiishi|en|Fukiishi|qid=Q11621213|lt=fukiishi|1=Fukiishi}}, protrusions on both sides of the constriction, and parts of a double moat have been confirmed. Another kofun similarly destroyed during the construction of Heijō Palace is the {{ill|Shimmeino Kofun|ja|神明野古墳|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Shimmeino Kofun|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid}}, located beneath the site of the second Daigokuden, with a length of 117 meters. The central portion of the rear mound is managed by the Imperial Household Agency as the tomb of Emperor Heizei. == built == Cylindrical {{ill|Haniwa|en|Haniwa|qid=Q849376|lt=haniwa|1=Haniwa}} and animal haniwa have been unearthed. The kofun is believed to have been constructed in the early 5th century, during the middle Kofun period. == Other notes == It is evident that the Ichiniwa Kofun predates the construction of Heijō-kyō and was not built as a tomb for Emperor Heizei, who lived during the Heian period. This has led to widespread skepticism about its Historic Site as his tomb. If the current Historic Site is accurate, Emperor Heizei would have been interred in an existing kofun rather than a new tomb being built for him. Haruna Hiroaki, who authored a biography of Emperor Heizei, has suggested this possibility.{{Cite book|author=Haruna Hiroaki|title=Heizei Tennō (Jinbutsu Sōsho)|publisher=Yoshikawa Kōbunkan|year=2009|pages=242–243}} == References == {{Reflist}} == Bibliography == * "Yamato Kofun I," Kashihara Archaeological Institute, 1997, {{ill|Jinbun Shoin|ja|人文書院|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Jinbun Shoin|1=Jimbun Shoin|comment=changed title based on wikidata|qid=Q11376034}}. == External Links == {{osm box|n|2575836153}} * [https://www.kunaicho.go.jp/ryobo/guide/051/index.html Yamamomo no misasagi] - {{ill|Imperial Household Agency|en|Imperial Household Agency|qid=Q1143602|lt=Imperial Household Agency|1=Imperial Household Agency}} {{japanese-history-stub}} 市庭古墳]] {{Kofun navbar}} {{translated page|ja|市庭古墳|version=103787547|comment=Imported full ja history}} [[ja:市庭古墳]] {{Commons category|Ichiniwa Kofun}} {{wikidata link|Q11480471}} [[Category:Emperor Heizei]] [[Category:Historic Saho-Saki Road]] [[Category:History of Nara]] [[Category:Japanese imperial tombs]] [[Category:Kofun of Nara prefecture]] [[Category:Pages using infobox ancient site with unknown parameters]] [[Category:Saki Tatenami Kofun group]] [[Category:Wikidata no short description]] [[Category:Zenpokoenfun]]
